translations.idiomatic: He shouted he would join ancient martyrs and doubted the court's survival.

sentences.zh: 」上意乃解。

translations.literal: ' The emperor's intent thereupon relented.'
translations.idiomatic: The emperor relented.

sentences.zh: 及後當修檻，上曰「勿易」，因而輯之，以旌直臣。

translations.literal: Later when they were to repair the railing, the emperor said 'Do not replace it' and thereupon patched it, thereby displaying the straight minister.
translations.idiomatic: Chengdi left the broken rail as a monument to blunt loyalty.

sentences.zh: 帝以延儒生，常特宴見。

translations.literal: Because Yan was a Confucian scholar, the emperor often gave him special feasts and audiences.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Huan often feasted Yuan Yan alone as a scholar-favorite.

sentences.zh: 時太史令上言客星經帝坐，帝密以問延。

translations.literal: At that time the grand astrologer submitted that a guest star had crossed the emperor's seat; the emperor secretly asked Yan about it.
translations.idiomatic: When a stray star crossed the throne asterism, the emperor quietly asked Yuan Yan what it meant.

sentences.zh: 延因上封事曰：「臣聞天子尊無為上，故天以為子，位臨臣庶，威重四海。

translations.literal: Yan thereupon submitted a sealed memorial, saying: 'I have heard the Son of Heaven is honored as none above—therefore Heaven makes him son; his position overlooks ministers and commoners; his awesome might fills the four seas.'
translations.idiomatic: Yuan Yan answered in a sealed note on the Son of Heaven's cosmic role.

sentences.zh: 動靜以禮，則星辰順序；

translations.literal: When movement and stillness follow ritual, then the stars march in order;
translations.idiomatic: Right conduct keeps the constellations in harmony;

sentences.zh: 意有邪僻，則晷度錯違。

translations.literal: if the mind harbors depravity, then the gnomon's degrees miss and violate.
translations.idiomatic: wicked intent throws the calendar and omens out of true.

sentences.zh: 陛下以河南尹鄧萬有龍潛之舊，封為通侯，恩重公卿，惠豐宗室。

translations.literal: Your Majesty, because metropolitan governor of Henan Deng Wan had old ties from before your accession, enfeoffed him as full marquis—favor heavier than the dukes and ministers, bounty rich for the imperial house.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Huan ennobled Deng Wan, an old companion from before the throne, beyond normal ministerial favor.

sentences.zh: 加頃引見，與之對博，上下媟黷，有虧尊嚴。

translations.literal: Further you lately summoned him to meet and played liubo with him; high and low were lewd and defiled, injuring august dignity.
translations.idiomatic: He added that gambling with Wan in public shamed the throne.

sentences.zh: 臣聞之，帝左右者，所以咨政德也。

translations.literal: Your servant has heard that those at the emperor's left and right are the means to consult on virtue in government.
translations.idiomatic: Court companions should refine policy, not dice games.

sentences.zh: 故周公戒成王曰『其朋其朋』，言慎所與也。

translations.literal: Therefore the Duke of Zhou warned King Cheng, saying 'Be careful of your companions, be careful of your companions'—meaning be careful whom you befriend.
translations.idiomatic: He quoted the Documents' warning about friends of the ruler.

sentences.zh: [一]昔宋閔公與強臣共博，列婦人於側，積此無禮，以致大□。

translations.literal: [1] Formerly Duke Min of Song played liubo with a powerful minister, ranged women at the side—accumulating such lack of ritual led to great disaster.'
translations.idiomatic: [1] Duke Min of Song's fatal dice game illustrated the danger.

sentences.zh: [二]武帝與幸臣李延年﹑韓嫣同臥起，尊爵重賜，情慾無猒，遂生驕淫之心，行不義之事，卒延年被戮，嫣伏其辜。

translations.literal: [2] Emperor Wu slept and rose with favored ministers Li Yannian and Han Yan, honored them with noble rank and heavy gifts, lust and appetite insatiable—thereupon arose arrogant and dissolute hearts, unrighteous deeds were done, and in the end Yannian was executed and Yan submitted to his crime.'
translations.idiomatic: [2] Emperor Wu's intimacy with singers ended in executions.

sentences.zh: [三]夫愛之則不覺其過，惡之則不知其善，所以事多放濫，物情生怨。

translations.literal: [3] When you love them you do not notice their faults; when you hate them you do not know their good—therefore affairs mostly run wild and feeling breeds resentment.'
translations.idiomatic: [3] Passion blinds rulers to fault and merit alike.

sentences.zh: 故王者賞人必酬其功，爵人必甄其德。

translations.literal: Therefore the true king rewards men only in repayment for their merit, and ennobles men only after discerning their virtue.'
translations.idiomatic: True kings match rank to proven virtue and service.

sentences.zh: [四]善人同處，則日聞嘉訓；

translations.literal: [4] When good men dwell together, daily they hear fine instruction;
translations.idiomatic: [4] Good company improves the ruler daily;

sentences.zh: 惡人從游，則日生邪情。

translations.literal: when evil men follow in one's travels, daily evil feeling is born.'
translations.idiomatic: evil company corrupts him.

sentences.zh: 孔子曰：『益者三友，損者三友。

translations.literal: Confucius said: 'There are three friendships that profit and three that harm.'
translations.idiomatic: He quoted Confucius on the three good and three bad friendships.

sentences.zh: 』[五]邪臣惑君，亂妾危主，以非所言則悅於耳，以非所行則翫於目，故令人君不能遠之。

translations.literal: ' [5] Wicked ministers delude the ruler, disorderly concubines endanger the lord—what is not proper speech pleases the ear, what is not proper conduct pleases the eye—therefore they make the lord unable to keep them at a distance.'
translations.idiomatic: [5] Flatterers and palace women ensnare the sovereign's senses.

sentences.zh: 仲尼曰：『唯女子與小人為難養，近之則不遜，遠之則怨。

translations.literal: Zhongni said: 'Only women and petty men are hard to nurture; close them and they are not deferential, distant them and they complain.'
translations.idiomatic: He cited Confucius on the difficulty of managing women and petty men.

sentences.zh: 』蓋聖人之明戒也！

translations.literal: ' This is the sage's clear warning!'
translations.idiomatic: That was the sage's blunt warning.

sentences.zh: 昔光武皇帝與嚴光俱寢，上天之異，其夕即見。

translations.literal: Formerly Emperor Guangwu slept together with Yan Guang; Heaven's anomaly that very night appeared.
translations.idiomatic: Even Guangwu sleeping with recluse Yan Guang drew a heavenly portent.

sentences.zh: [六]夫以光武之聖德，嚴光之高賢，君臣合道，尚降此變，豈況陛下今所親幸，以賤為貴，以卑為尊哉？

translations.literal: [6] With Guangwu's sagely virtue and Yan Guang's lofty worth, ruler and minister matched the Way—still this change descended—how much more Your Majesty's present intimates, making the low noble and the humble honored!'
translations.idiomatic: [6] If even that virtuous pair drew omens, how much riskier were Huan's low-born favorites?

sentences.zh: 惟陛下遠讒諛之人，納謇謇之士，除左右之權，寤宦官之敝。

translations.literal: Only may Your Majesty keep far from slanderous flatterers, admit bluntly honest scholars, remove power from those at your side, and awaken to the harm of eunuchs.
translations.idiomatic: He begged Huan to shun flatterers, hear upright men, and clip eunuch power.

sentences.zh: 使積善日熙，[七]佞惡消殄，則干□可除。

translations.literal: Let accumulated good daily shine; [7] let wicked flattery be extinguished—then portents of drought may be removed.'
translations.idiomatic: [7] Virtue would dispel the drought omens.

sentences.zh: 」帝省其奏。

translations.literal: ' The emperor read his memorial.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Huan read the memorial.

sentences.zh: 因以病自上，乞骸骨還家。

translations.literal: Thereupon he pleaded illness on his own behalf, begging bones to return home.
translations.idiomatic: Yuan Yan then resigned on grounds of illness.

sentences.zh: 靈帝復特徵，不行，病卒。

translations.literal: Emperor Ling later specially summoned him; he did not go, and died of illness.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Ling called him again; he stayed home and died.

sentences.zh: 注[一]尚書周公戒成王曰：「孺子其朋，孺子其朋，慎其往！」

translations.literal: Note [1] The Shang shu: the Duke of Zhou warned King Cheng, saying 'Young man, beware your companions, beware your companions, be careful where you go!'"
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [1]: The Documents passage on companions is cited.

sentences.zh: 注[二]公羊經書「宋萬弒其君捷」。

translations.literal: Note [2] The Gongyang classic records 'Song Wan assassinated his lord Jie.'"
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [2]: Song Wan's crime heads the Gongyang note.

sentences.zh: 傳曰：「宋萬嘗與魯莊公戰，獲於莊公，歸捨諸宮中，數月然後歸之。

translations.literal: The commentary says: 'Song Wan once fought Duke Zhuang of Lu, was captured by Zhuang, lodged in the palace several months, then was returned.'
translations.idiomatic: Song Wan had been Lu's prisoner before serving Song.

sentences.zh: 與宋閔公博，婦人在側，萬曰：『甚矣魯侯之淑，魯侯之美！

translations.literal: He played liubo with Duke Min of Song; a woman stood at the side; Wan said: 'How perfect is the lord of Lu—how beautiful the lord of Lu!'
translations.idiomatic: At dice Wan praised Duke Zhuang of Lu while a woman watched.

sentences.zh: 天下諸侯宜為君者唯魯侯爾。

translations.literal: Of all the feudal lords only the lord of Lu should be ruler.'"
translations.idiomatic: Wan said only Lu's duke deserved the throne.

sentences.zh: 』閔公矜此婦人，妒其言，顧曰：『此虜也，魯侯之美惡乎至？

translations.literal: ' Duke Min, priding himself on this woman, envied the words and looked back, saying: 'This is a captive—how could the lord of Lu's beauty reach that?'
translations.idiomatic: Min jealously sneered that Wan was a prisoner praising Lu.

sentences.zh: 』萬怒，搏閔公，絕其脰。」

translations.literal: ' Wan grew angry, struck Duke Min, and broke his neck.'"
translations.idiomatic: Wan murdered Min in rage.

sentences.zh: 注[三]李延年，中山人也。

translations.literal: Note [3] Li Yannian was a man of Zhongshan.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [3]: Li Yannian's origin.

sentences.zh: 身及父母兄弟皆故倡人也。

translations.literal: He and his parents and brothers had all been entertainers.
translations.idiomatic: His whole family were performers.

sentences.zh: 武帝時，延年女弟得幸，號曰李夫人。

translations.literal: In Emperor Wu's time his younger sister won favor—she was titled Lady Li.
translations.idiomatic: His sister became Lady Li.

sentences.zh: 延年善歌舞，為協律都尉，佩二千石印綬，與上臥起。

translations.literal: Yannian was skilled at songs and dances, became director of harmony, wore a two-thousand-bushel seal ribbon, and slept and rose with the emperor.
translations.idiomatic: He rose to music director with ministerial rank and shared the emperor's bed.

sentences.zh: 弟季與中人亂，出入驕恣，上遂誅延年兄弟。

translations.literal: His younger brother Ji debauched with a palace man, came and went arrogantly; the emperor thereupon executed Yannian and his brothers.
translations.idiomatic: His brother's scandal cost the whole family their lives.

sentences.zh: 韓嫣，韓王信之曾孫也。

translations.literal: Han Yan was the great-grandson of King Xin of Han.
translations.idiomatic: Han Yan descended from Han Xin.

sentences.zh: 武帝為王時，與嫣相愛，後位至上大夫，賞賜擬鄧通，與上臥起，出入永巷，以奸聞被誅。

translations.literal: When Emperor Wu was a king he loved Yan; later his rank reached senior grandee; rewards rivaled Deng Tong's; he slept and rose with the emperor, went in and out of the long lanes, and was executed when his debauchery was heard.
translations.idiomatic: Yan had been Wu's boyhood favorite until scandal brought execution.

sentences.zh: 注[四]甄，明也。

translations.literal: Note [4] Zhen means 'discern clearly.'
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [4]: Zhen means to judge clearly.

sentences.zh: 注[五]論語孔子曰：「友直，友諒，友多聞，益矣。

translations.literal: Note [5] The Analects: Confucius said, 'Befriend the upright, the trustworthy, the broadly heard—this profits.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [5]: Confucius lists three good friends.

sentences.zh: 友便僻，友善柔，友便佞，損矣。」

translations.literal: Befriend the slick, the soft, the glib—this harms.'"
translations.idiomatic: Befriend the devious, the ingratiating, and the glib, and you will suffer harm—these are the three kinds of friends that injure you.

sentences.zh: 注[六]事見逸人傳。

translations.literal: Note [6] The matter appears in the Tradition of Recluses.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [6]: Yan Guang's story is in the recluse tradition.

sentences.zh: 注[七]熙，廣也。

translations.literal: Note [7] Xi means 'broad.'
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [7]: Xi glosses as broad or spreading.

sentences.zh: 子驥，白馬令，亦稱善士。

translations.literal: His son Ji was magistrate of White Horse and was also called a good gentleman.
translations.idiomatic: His son Yuan Ji governed Baima and won a good name.
Endnote marker.

sentences.zh: 注[一]謝承書曰興字驥。

translations.literal: Note [1] Xie Cheng's book says Xing styled Ji.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [1]: Xie Cheng gives the son's style as Ji.

sentences.zh: 徐璆字孟玉，[一]廣陵海西人也。

translations.literal: Xu Qiu, courtesy name Mengyu, [1] was a man of Haixi in Guangling.
translations.idiomatic: Xu Qiu, styled Mengyu, came from Haixi in Guangling.

sentences.zh: 父淑，度遼將軍，有名於邊。

translations.literal: His father Shu was general who crosses the Liao and was famed on the frontier.
translations.idiomatic: His father Xu Shu was the famous frontier general.

sentences.zh: [二]璆少博學，辟公府，舉高第。

translations.literal: [2] Qiu in youth was broadly learned and was summoned to the ducal offices, recommended of highest rank.
translations.idiomatic: [2] Young Xu Qiu read widely, entered the bureaucracy, and ranked top in evaluation.

sentences.zh: [三]稍遷荊州刺史。

translations.literal: [3] He was gradually promoted regional inspector of Jingzhou.
translations.idiomatic: [3] He rose to regional inspector of Jingzhou.

sentences.zh: 時董太后姊子張忠為南陽太守，因埶放濫，臧罪數億。

translations.literal: At that time Empress Dowager Dong's sister's son Zhang Zhong was grand administrator of Nanyang; relying on influence he acted wildly and his embezzlement ran to several hundred million.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Zhong, the empress's nephew, looted Nanyang.

sentences.zh: 璆臨當之部，太后遣中常侍以忠屬璆。

translations.literal: When Qiu was about to take up his post, the empress dowager sent a regular palace attendant to entrust Zhong to Qiu.
translations.idiomatic: The empress dowager sent a eunuch to ask Qiu to spare Zhong.

sentences.zh: 璆對曰：「臣身為國，不敢聞命。

translations.literal: Qiu answered: 'Your servant's person belongs to the state—I dare not heed private orders.'
translations.idiomatic: Xu Qiu refused to obey private influence.

sentences.zh: 」太后怒，遽征忠為司隸校尉，以相威臨。

translations.literal: ' The empress dowager grew angry and hastily summoned Zhong as metropolitan commandant to threaten him in turn.
translations.idiomatic: She retaliated by promoting Zhong to metropolitan commandant over Qiu.

sentences.zh: 璆到州，舉奏忠臧余一億，使冠軍縣上簿詣大司農，以彰暴其事。

translations.literal: When Qiu reached the province he impeached Zhong for embezzlement of more than a hundred million cash, ordered Champion county to forward the ledgers to the grand minister of agriculture, and thereby exposed the affair.
translations.idiomatic: Qiu impeached Zhong anyway and sent the books to the minister of finance.

sentences.zh: 又奏五郡太守及屬縣有臧污者，悉征案罪，威風大行。

translations.literal: Further he impeached five commandery grand administrators and subordinate counties that had corruption, all summoning and investigating guilt—awesome wind went greatly.
translations.idiomatic: He purged five corrupt prefects and their counties.

sentences.zh: 中平元年，與中郎將朱儁擊黃巾賊於宛，破之。

translations.literal: In the first year of Zhongping he with colonel Zhu Jun attacked Yellow Turban bandits at Wan and defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: In Zhongping 1 he joined Zhu Jun to crush rebels at Wan.

sentences.zh: 張忠怨璆，與諸閹官構造無端，璆遂以罪征。

translations.literal: Zhang Zhong resented Qiu and with various eunuchs fabricated baseless charges; Qiu thereupon was summoned for crime.
translations.idiomatic: Zhong and eunuchs framed Xu Qiu.

sentences.zh: 有破賊功，得免官歸家。

translations.literal: Because he had merit in breaking the bandits he was allowed to dismiss office and return home.
translations.idiomatic: His military merit won him dismissal instead of worse punishment.

sentences.zh: 後再征，遷汝南太守，轉東海相，所在化行。

translations.literal: Later twice summoned, he was promoted grand administrator of Runan, transferred chancellor of Donghai—wherever he went transformation proceeded.
translations.idiomatic: He later governed Runan and Donghai with the same transforming touch.

sentences.zh: 注[一]璆音仇。

translations.literal: Note [1] Qiu is read like qiu.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [1]: Fanqie note on the name.

sentences.zh: 注[二]謝承書曰：「淑字伯進，寬裕*

translations.literal: Note [2] Xie Cheng's book says: 'Shu styled Bojin, was generous [lacuna]
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [2]: Xie Cheng begins praise of Xu Shu—

translations.literal: (zhuan)
translations.idiomatic: The manuscript inserts the graph zhuan in parentheses.

sentences.zh: **[博]*學，習孟氏易、春秋公羊傳、禮記、周官。

translations.literal: **[bo]* learning, studied the Meng family Changes, Gongyang Chunqiu, Record of Rites, and Offices of Zhou.'
translations.idiomatic: —calling him learned in the classics and military texts.

sentences.zh: 善誦太公六韜，交接英雄，常有壯志。」

translations.literal: ' He was skilled at reciting the Grand Duke's Six Secret Teachings and made friends with heroes—he always had lofty ambition.'"
translations.idiomatic: He knew the Tai Gong's six arts and befriended bold men.

sentences.zh: 注[三]袁山松書曰：「璆少履清高，立朝正色。

translations.literal: Note [3] Yuan Shansong's book says: 'Qiu in youth trod a pure and lofty path and stood in court with stern color.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [3]: Yuan Shansong praises his integrity.

sentences.zh: 稱揚後進，惟恐不及。」

translations.literal: He praised and raised later talents, fearing only that he could not reach them enough.'"
translations.idiomatic: He promoted juniors zealously.

sentences.zh: 獻帝遷許，以廷尉征，當詣京師，道為袁術所劫，授璆以上公之位。

translations.literal: When Emperor Xian moved to Xu he was summoned as commandant of justice; on the way to the capital he was seized by Yuan Shu on the road, who offered Qiu the rank of high duke.
translations.idiomatic: En route to Xu, Yuan Shu kidnapped him and offered a princely title.

sentences.zh: 璆乃歎曰：「龔勝、鮑宣，獨何人哉？

translations.literal: Qiu thereupon sighed: 'Gong Sheng, Bao Xuan—what manner of men were they?
translations.idiomatic: Xu Qiu invoked the Han martyrs Gong Sheng and Bao Xuan.

sentences.zh: 守之必死！

translations.literal: To keep faith is surely to die!'
translations.idiomatic: He swore he would die before yielding.

sentences.zh: 」[一]術不敢逼。

translations.literal: ' [1] Shu did not dare force him.'
translations.idiomatic: [1] Yuan Shu dared not press him.

sentences.zh: 術死軍破，璆得其盜國璽，及還許，上之，[二]並送前所假汝南、東海二郡印綬。

translations.literal: When Shu died and his army broke, Qiu obtained the usurped state seal he had stolen; when he returned to Xu he presented it, [2] and together returned the borrowed seals of Runan and Donghai commanderies.
translations.idiomatic: After Shu's fall Qiu brought the imperial seal and borrowed seals back to Xu.

sentences.zh: 司徒趙溫謂璆曰：「君遭大難，猶存此邪？

translations.literal: Director of works Zhao Wen said to Qiu: 'You met great disaster—yet you still keep this?'
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Wen marveled that he still carried the seal.

sentences.zh: 」璆曰：「昔蘇武困於匈奴，不隊七尺之節，況此方寸印乎？」

translations.literal: ' Qiu said: 'Formerly Su Wu was trapped among the Xiongnu yet did not drop his seven-foot tassel—how much more this square-inch seal?'"
translations.idiomatic: He compared himself to Su Wu clutching his Han credentials.

sentences.zh: 後拜太常，使持節拜曹操為丞相。

translations.literal: Later he was appointed grand master of ceremonies with staff of authority and invested Cao Cao as chancellor.
translations.idiomatic: He later carried the staff to appoint Cao Cao chancellor.

sentences.zh: 操以相讓璆，璆不敢當。

translations.literal: Cao offered the chancellorship to Qiu; Qiu did not dare accept.
translations.idiomatic: Cao Cao offered him the post; he refused.

sentences.zh: 卒於官。

translations.literal: He died in office.
translations.idiomatic: He died in office.

sentences.zh: 論曰：孫懿以高明見忌，而受欺於陰計；

translations.literal: The judgment says: Sun Yi was jealoused for his brilliance and deceived by a secret plot;
translations.idiomatic: The historian judges Sun Yi credulous, Zhai Pu wily,

sentences.zh: 翟酺資譎數取通，而終之以謇諫。

translations.literal: Zhai Pu used craft to advance yet ended in blunt remonstrance.
translations.idiomatic: yet Zhai ended as a straight speaker.

sentences.zh: 豈性智自有周偏，先後之要殊度乎？

translations.literal: Is it that nature and wit are uneven by themselves, and the key of sequence differs in measure?
translations.idiomatic: Perhaps timing and temperament simply differ.

sentences.zh: 應氏七世才聞，而奉、劭采章為盛。

translations.literal: For seven generations the Ying house produced talent, yet Feng and Shao were brightest in compiling law.
translations.idiomatic: The Ying clan shone for seven generations, especially Feng and Shao.

sentences.zh: 及撰著篇籍，甄紀異知，雖雲小道，亦有可觀者焉。

translations.literal: When they wrote books and arranged strange knowledge, though called minor paths, still there is something to view.
translations.idiomatic: Their reference works, though minor genres, reward reading.

sentences.zh: 延、璆應對辯正，而不*

translations.literal: Yan and Qiu answered with debate straight yet did not [lacuna]
translations.idiomatic: Yuan Yan and Xu Qiu spoke truth to power without—

translations.literal: (ke)
translations.idiomatic: The text marks a damaged graph read ke.

sentences.zh: *犯陵上之尤，斯固辭之不可以已也。

translations.literal: *commit the fault of offending a superior—thus indeed remonstrance cannot stop.'
translations.idiomatic: —without crossing into lethal insult; remonstrance could not be abandoned.
Endnote marker.

sentences.zh: 注[一]左氏傳孔子曰：「辭之不可以已也如是夫！

translations.literal: Note [1] The Zuo Tradition: Confucius said, 'How remonstrance cannot be dropped—like this!'
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [1]: Confucius praised Zichan's unstoppable counsel.

sentences.zh: 子產有辭，諸侯賴之。」

translations.literal: Zichan had words—the feudal lords relied on him.'"
translations.idiomatic: Zichan's eloquence steadied the age.

sentences.zh: 贊曰：楊終、李法，華陽有聞。

translations.literal: The encomium says: Yang Zhong and Li Fa had fame in Huayang.
translations.idiomatic: The eulogy praises Yang Zhong and Li Fa in the west.

sentences.zh: [一]二應克聰，亦表汝濆。

translations.literal: [1] The two Yings were keenly clever and also shone by the Ru ford.
translations.idiomatic: [1] The two Yings likewise glittered along the Ru River.

sentences.zh: [二]翟酺詐懿，霍諝請舅。

translations.literal: [2] Zhai Pu cheated Sun Yi; Huo Xu pleaded for his uncle.
translations.idiomatic: [2] Zhai tricked Sun; Huo pleaded for Song Guang.

sentences.zh: 延能訐帝，璆亦啎後。

translations.literal: Yan could rebuke the emperor; Qiu also defied an empress.
translations.idiomatic: Yuan Yan faced the emperor; Xu Qiu defied empress pressure.

sentences.zh: 注[一]益州，古梁州之域。

translations.literal: Note [1] Yizhou is the old domain of Liangzhou.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [1]: Yizhou corresponds to ancient Liangzhou.

sentences.zh: 尚書曰：「華陽黑水惟梁州。

translations.literal: The Shang shu says: 'South of Huayang, north of Blackwater—that is Liangzhou.'"
translations.idiomatic: The Documents defines Liangzhou by Huayang and Blackwater.

sentences.zh: 」孔安國注曰：「北拒華山之陽，南拒黑水。

translations.literal: Kong Anguo's commentary says: 'North reaching the sunny side of Mount Hua, south reaching Blackwater.'"
translations.idiomatic: Kong Anguo glosses its bounds.

sentences.zh: 」故常璩□蜀事而謂之華陽國志焉。

translations.literal: ' Therefore Chang Qu [lacuna] Shu affairs and called his book the Record of Huayang States.'
translations.idiomatic: Hence Chang Qu titled his regional history Huayang guo zhi.

sentences.zh: 注[二]鄭玄注周禮曰：「水涯曰濆。」

translations.literal: Note [2] Zheng Xuan's commentary on the Zhou li says: 'A river bank is called fen.'"
translations.idiomatic: Commentary [2]: Fen means a riverbank—here the Ru ford.

sentences.zh: 校勘記

translations.literal: Collation notes
translations.idiomatic: The heading marks the collation section.

sentences.zh: 一五九七頁一二行民懷土思群書治要「民」作「人」。

translations.literal: Page 1597 line 12: the Qunshu zhiyao writes ren for min in the line about longing for home.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: variant ren for min in an quoted line.

sentences.zh: 按：作「人」是，此蓋後人回改而誤者。

translations.literal: Commentary: writing ren is correct; this is probably a later mistaken emendation back to min.
translations.idiomatic: The editor prefers ren.

sentences.zh: 一六〇〇頁二行豈可不臨深履薄以為至戒按：王先謙謂末有復語，疑此衍文。

translations.literal: Page 1600 line 2: Wang Xianqian suspects repetitive wording in the treading-ice warning.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: possible redundant phrase in Yang Zhong's letter.

sentences.zh: 一六〇〇頁四行鑒念前往按：殿本「往」作「世」。

translations.literal: Page 1600 line 4: the palace edition writes shi for wang in jian nian qian wang.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: variant graph in Ma Liao warning.

sentences.zh: 一六〇〇頁八行晉侯殺其太子申生至直稱君者甚之也按：章懷引經傳多刪節，此注所引，與公羊傳原文更多出入。

translations.literal: Page 1600 line 8: from Jin marquis killing crown prince Shensheng through 'calling the ruler alone shows heavy blame'—commentary: Zhang Xuan's quotations from Gongyang are often abbreviated and differ from the received Gongyang text.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: Zhang's Gongyang quotes are abridged versus the received classic.

sentences.zh: 公羊傳原文作「晉侯殺其世子申生。

translations.literal: The Gongyang original text reads 'The marquis of Jin killed his heir apparent Shensheng.'
translations.idiomatic: Collation: the received Gongyang wording on Jin and Shensheng.

sentences.zh: 曷為直稱晉侯以殺？

translations.literal: Why is the marquis of Jin named straight for the killing?
translations.idiomatic: Collation continues the Gongyang question.

sentences.zh: 殺世子母弟直稱君者甚之也」。

translations.literal: Killing heir or younger brother by full blood while naming the ruler alone shows heavy blame.'"
translations.idiomatic: Collation cites the Gongyang answer on naming the ruler.

sentences.zh: 一六〇〇頁一一行廖子防及光俱為黃門郎按：沉家本謂光、防乃廖弟，非廖子，注謬。

translations.literal: Page 1611 line 1: Shen Jiaben notes that Fang and Guang were Ma Liao's younger brothers, not his sons—the commentary is wrong.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: Ma Fang and Guang were brothers of Liao, not sons.

sentences.zh: 此傳上文言廖不訓諸子，下文言廖不納，子豫後坐縣書誹謗，廖以就國，則終所稱黃門郎即指廖子豫，廖傳不言豫為黃門郎，史文不具耳。

translations.literal: The biography above says Liao did not instruct his sons; below it says Liao did not accept advice, and son Yu later was convicted—so the yellow-gate gentlemen Yang Zhong meant were Ma Yu; Ma's biography does not say Yu was a yellow-gate—the text is simply incomplete.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: reconciles yellow-gate reference with Ma Yu.

sentences.zh: 下文「視成任性」注引馬防傳云云，亦誤。

translations.literal: The note below on shi cheng ren xing citing Ma Fang's tradition is also mistaken.
translations.idiomatic: Collation flags another commentary error.

sentences.zh: 一六〇〇頁一二行選長者之有節行者與之居按：史記外戚傳作「選長者士之有節行者與居」。

translations.literal: Page 1611 line 12: the Shiji outer kin biography reads 'choose elders among gentlemen of principle to dwell with them.'
translations.idiomatic: Collation: Shiji variant with shi inserted.

sentences.zh: 一六〇三頁七行此最安危*[之極]*戒據汲本、殿本補。

translations.literal: Page 1603 line 7: this most touches safety and peril—the ji of ji is supplied from Ji and Palace editions.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: textual restoration of ji.

sentences.zh: 一六〇四頁二行權並族害*

translations.literal: Page 1604 line 2: power pools in kin and harms one another*
translations.idiomatic: Collation: damaged line in apocryphon—

translations.literal: (shi)
translations.idiomatic: —gloss inserts shi.

sentences.zh: **[己]*奸行據汲本、殿本改。

translations.literal: **[ji]* wicked conduct—emended per Ji and Palace editions.
translations.idiomatic: —editors restore ji for wicked conduct.

sentences.zh: 一六〇四頁八行斂天下之財按：「天」下原脫「下」字，逕據汲本、殿本補。

translations.literal: Page 1604 line 8: gathering the realm's wealth—the character xia after tian was missing and is supplied from editions.
translations.idiomatic: Collation adds missing graph in Zhai Pu memorial.

sentences.zh: 一六〇五頁六行叛羌千餘騎徙敦煌來鈔郡界按：刊誤謂案文「徙」當作「從」。

translations.literal: Page 1605 line 6: rebel Qiang thousand riders from Dunhuang—the Kanwu says xi should read cong.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: xi should be cong meaning 'from.'

sentences.zh: 一六〇五頁一三行*

translations.literal: Page 1605 line 13: asterisk editorial mark.
translations.idiomatic: Collation marks asterisk in commentary.

sentences.zh: (宜升)

translations.literal: (yi sheng)
translations.idiomatic: Collation gloss yi sheng.

sentences.zh: **[斗]*歷改憲*[宜]*行先王至德要道校補引錢大昭說，謂「升」當作「斗」，見春秋保干圖。

translations.literal: Page 1605 line 13: dou calendar change—yi sheng line—Qian Daxin notes sheng should be dou per Chunqiu Baogan tu.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: dou for calendar omen.

sentences.zh: 校補謂案續志律歷中篇論歷，凡三引保干圖讖文，皆作「三百年斗歷改憲」。

translations.literal: The collation supplement says the Xu Han treatise on pitchpipes and calendar quotes the Baogan tu thrice with 'three hundred years, dou calendar change constitution.'
translations.idiomatic: Collation cites three parallel Baogan tu passages.

sentences.zh: 所謂斗歷者，即古法冬至日在建星，建星謂北斗也。

translations.literal: So-called dou calendar means the ancient method where winter solstice sun stood in Jianxing—Jianxing means the Dipper.
translations.idiomatic: Collation explains dou as Dipper-based reckoning.

sentences.zh: 歲十二月以配天之十二辰，取斗杓所指為驗，閏月無中氣，則北斗邪指兩辰之閒，以定四時而成歲。

translations.literal: The twelve months match Heaven's twelve branches; the Dipper handle points verify them; intercalary months without mid qi mean the Dipper handle skews between two branches to fix the four seasons and complete the year.
translations.idiomatic: Collation glosses intercalation via Dipper.

sentences.zh: 漢興迄章帝，改用四分歷，適當三百年，已應斗歷改憲之讖矣。

translations.literal: From Han's rise through Emperor Zhang the four-part calendar was adopted—right at three hundred years it already matched the dou calendar change omen.
translations.idiomatic: Collation links Han calendar reform to the prophecy.

sentences.zh: 輔本謂漢更有四百年之難，其數即起於三百年改憲之閒，宜豫修省，以銷其禍，則注引耆舊傳「宜」字，並當在「斗歷改憲」下也。

translations.literal: Fu editions say Han would face another four-hundred-year crisis beginning from the three-hundred-year calendar change—so the yi character in the Yibu qijiu zhuan quotation should stand below dou calendar change.
translations.idiomatic: Collation reorders yi in the apocryphon.

sentences.zh: 今據改。

translations.literal: Now emended accordingly.
translations.idiomatic: Collation states the emendation is adopted.

sentences.zh: 一六〇六頁一行上檄章救酺按：殿本考證王會汾謂上移下曰檄，此止可言上章耳，不應有「檄」字，明衍。

translations.literal: Page 1606 line 1: submitting xi zhang to save Pu—Wang Huifen says xi means upward and zhang downward; only shang zhang fits, xi is redundant.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: xi in memorial title is likely error.

sentences.zh: 一六〇六頁四行孝文皇帝始置一經博士汲本「一經」作「五經」。

translations.literal: Page 1606 line 4: Emperor Wen first established one-classics doctor—Ji edition reads five classics.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: one versus five classics debate.

sentences.zh: 惠校本作「一經」，惠所據乃北宋本也。

translations.literal: Hui's collation keeps one classic matching Northern Song.
translations.idiomatic: Hui edition supports one classic.

sentences.zh: 集解引周壽昌說，謂據王氏玉海引此，作「文帝始置一經博士」，殆宋本此書有作「一經」者，非「五經」也。

translations.literal: Collected notes cite Zhou Shouchang: Wang Yuhai quotes this as Wen establishing one-classics doctors—Northern Song had one classic, not five.
translations.idiomatic: Collation cites Wang Yuhai for one classic.

sentences.zh: 今按：證以章懷注，則作「五經」為合，作「一經」者，殆後人以文帝未嘗於五經□置博士而改之耳。

translations.literal: Now judged against Zhang's commentary, five classics fits; one classic is probably a later change because Wen had not yet set five-classics chairs.
translations.idiomatic: Editor prefers five classics reading.

sentences.zh: 一六〇七頁一一行行部四十二縣按：集解引錢大昕說，謂郡國志汝南郡領三十七城，此云「四十二」，未詳。

translations.literal: Page 1607 line 11: touring forty-two counties—Qian Daxin notes the commandery gazetteer lists only thirty-seven cities; forty-two is unexplained.
translations.idiomatic: The editor notes that when Ying Feng toured forty-two counties on circuit, Qian Daxin observed that the commandery gazetteer for Runan lists only thirty-seven walled towns, so the figure forty-two remains unexplained.

sentences.zh: 一六〇七頁一四行奉少為上計吏按：刊誤謂「吏」當作「史」。

translations.literal: Page 1607 line 14: Feng in youth was chief clerk on accounting—Kanwu says li should be shi.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: li versus shi for clerk title.

sentences.zh: 一六〇八頁一六行富*

translations.literal: Page 1608 line 16: fu*
translations.idiomatic: Collation damaged fu line—

translations.literal: (chen)
translations.idiomatic: —gloss chen.

sentences.zh: **[辰]*諫曰據汲本改。

translations.literal: **[chen]* remonstrates—emended per Ji edition.
translations.idiomatic: —restores minister Chen's name.

sentences.zh: 一六〇九頁一行喪婦之長女不娶為其不受命也按：李慈銘謂「喪婦」當作「喪父」。

translations.literal: Page 1609 line 1: eldest daughter of a widow not taken because she does not receive command—Li Ciming says sang fu should be sang fu meaning widowed father.
translations.idiomatic: Collation on five prohibitions wording.

sentences.zh: 今韓詩外傳無此文。

translations.literal: Now the Han shi waizhuan lacks this passage.
translations.idiomatic: Collation notes missing Han shi passage.

sentences.zh: 何氏公羊莊二十七年解詁與此略同，惟「為其不受命也」作「無教戒也」。

translations.literal: He Xiu's Gongyang Zhuang 27 commentary matches this roughly except 'does not receive command' reads 'lacks instruction.'
translations.idiomatic: Collation compares Gongyang subcommentary.

sentences.zh: 大戴禮本命篇又小異。

translations.literal: The Da Dai li Benming chapter differs slightly again.
translations.idiomatic: Collation cites Da Dai li variant.

sentences.zh: 一六〇九頁三行數萬言按：汲本作「數十萬言」。

translations.literal: Page 1609 line 3: several wan graphs—Ji edition reads several shi wan.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: tenfold figure variant.

sentences.zh: 一六〇九頁六行謝承書*

translations.literal: Page 1609 line 6: Xie Cheng's book*
translations.idiomatic: Collation break in Xie Cheng—

translations.literal: (yue)
translations.idiomatic: —insert yue.

sentences.zh: *應氏譜並雲字仲遠據刊誤刪。

translations.literal: *Ying genealogy both say styled Zhongyuan—deleted per Kanwu.
translations.idiomatic: Collation removes erroneous phrase.

sentences.zh: 一六〇九頁六行漢官儀又作*[仲]*瑗據汲本、殿本補。

translations.literal: Page 1609 line 6: Han guan yi again writes Zhongyuan—supplied from Ji and Palace.
translations.idiomatic: Collation restores graph in style name.

sentences.zh: 一六一一頁四行夫時化則刑重按：集解引錢大昕說，謂案漢書刑法志「治則刑重，亂則刑輕」。

translations.literal: Page 1611 line 4: when the age is transformed punishments are heavy—Qian Daxin cites Han shu penal treatise: order heavy in order, light in chaos.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: hua versus zhi Tang taboo issue.

sentences.zh: 此傳及注中「化」字本是「治」字，唐人諱治，故章懷注范史，多改「治」為「理」，亦有改為「化」者，「世」皆改為「代」，亦有改為「時」者，此傳下文「時輕時重」是也。

translations.literal: This biography and commentary hua graphs were originally zhi; Tang taboo changed zhi to li, also sometimes to hua; shi became dai, sometimes shi—this biography's 'sometimes light sometimes heavy' is an example.
translations.idiomatic: Editors explain that graphs read hua in this biography and its commentary originally stood for zhi (to govern); Tang dynasty taboo on the emperor's name replaced zhi with li (to order), sometimes with hua (to transform), while shi (age) became dai (generation) or occasionally shi (season)—which is why the line below reads 'punishments are at times light, at times heavy.'

sentences.zh: 一六一二頁八行顧由是無計慮耳按：汲本、殿本「由無」作「無由」。

translations.literal: Page 1612 line 14: gu you is wu ji lu er—Ji and Palace read wu you.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: word order in gloss.

sentences.zh: 一六一二頁一四行故膠*

translations.literal: Page 1612 line 14: therefore Jiao*
translations.idiomatic: Collation on Dong Zhongshu line—

translations.literal: (east)
translations.idiomatic: —east gloss.

sentences.zh: **[西]*相董仲舒按：集解引錢大昕說，謂「膠東」當作「膠西」。

translations.literal: **[west]* chancellor Dong Zhongshu—Qian Daxin says Jiaodong should be Jiaoxi.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: Jiaoxi not Jiaodong.

sentences.zh: 今據改。

translations.literal: Now emended accordingly.
translations.idiomatic: Collation adopts Jiaoxi.

sentences.zh: 一六一三頁六行斯文之族按：汲本「族」作「俗」。

translations.literal: Page 1613 line 6: si wen zhi zu—Ji edition reads su.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: zu versus su.

sentences.zh: 一六一四頁一一行惟*

translations.literal: Page 1614 line 11: wei*
translations.idiomatic: Collation lacuna wei—

translations.literal: (cui)
translations.idiomatic: —cui gloss.

sentences.zh: **[悴]*古字通據汲本、殿本改。

translations.literal: **[cui]* interchangeable ancient graphs—emended per Ji and Palace.
translations.idiomatic: Collation restores cui.

sentences.zh: 一六一四頁一五行釋時俗嫌疑按：汲本「釋」作「識」。

translations.literal: Page 1614 line 15: explaining vulgar doubts—Ji edition reads recognizing.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: shi versus shi variant.

sentences.zh: 一六一四頁一六行皆傳於時按：「於」原作「乎」，逕據汲本、殿本改。

translations.literal: Page 1614 line 16: all circulated in his time—yu was originally hu, emended per editions.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: yu versus hu preposition.

sentences.zh: 一六一五頁一行弟子瑒按：原本正文及注「瑒」字皆斗「宜」，各本不誤，逕改正。

translations.literal: Page 1615 line 1: disciple Yang—original text and notes wrote wrong graph for Yang, editions correct.
translations.idiomatic: Collation fixes Yang graph.

sentences.zh: 一六一五頁二行瑒字德璉按：原於「璉」作「□」，□不成字，據汲本、殿本徑改正。

translations.literal: Page 1615 line 2: Yang styled Deqian—original had lacuna on lian, corrected per editions.
translations.idiomatic: Collation fixes Deqian graph.

sentences.zh: 一六一六頁一五行謂遵依常轍按：「謂」原斗「論」，「勂」原斗「徹」，逕據汲本、殿本改正。

translations.literal: Page 1616 line 15: meaning follow usual rut—wei was dou lun, che was dou che, corrected per editions.
translations.idiomatic: Collation fixes two wrong graphs in note.

sentences.zh: 一六一七頁一行不食烏喙按：「喙」原斗「啄」，逕據汲本、殿本改正。

translations.literal: Page 1617 line 1: not eating wuhui—hui was dou zhuo, corrected per editions.
translations.idiomatic: Collation fixes aconite graph.

sentences.zh: 下同。

translations.literal: Same below.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: same fix below.

sentences.zh: 一六一七頁六行令容駟馬蓋車按：「令」原斗「今」，逕據汲本、殿本改正。

translations.literal: Page 1617 line 6: order the gate to admit four-horse carriage—ling was dou jin, corrected per editions.
translations.idiomatic: Collation fixes ling graph.

sentences.zh: 一六一七頁一三行子鑈按：汲本、殿本「鑈」作「雋」。

translations.literal: Page 1617 line 13: son Jun—Ji and Palace read Jun as Xun for the son's name.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: son's name Jun versus Xun.

sentences.zh: 一六一八頁三行在事二年按：汲本、殿本「二」作「三」。

translations.literal: Page 1618 line 3: in office two years—Ji and Palace read three years.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: two versus three years.

sentences.zh: 一六一八頁七行尚書令陳蕃任事則化按：御覽四二七、四五二引，「化」並作「治」，此亦避唐諱改。

translations.literal: Page 1618 line 7: Minister Chen Fan handling affairs then zhi—Yulan citations read zhi as order, another Tang taboo change.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: hua for zhi in Yuan Yan memorial.

sentences.zh: 一六一八頁八行昔朱雲廷折欄檻按：刊誤謂案文「廷」下少「爭」字。

translations.literal: Page 1618 line 8: formerly Zhu Yun in court broke the railing—Kanwu says ting lacks zheng.
translations.idiomatic: Collation adds zheng to tingzheng.

sentences.zh: 一六一九頁二行河南尹鄧萬按：集解引王補說，謂通鑒作「鄧萬世」，本書鄧後、陳蕃傳引並作「鄧萬世」。

translations.literal: Page 1619 line 2: metropolitan governor Deng Wan—Wang Bu cites Zizhi tongjian as Deng Wanshi; empress and Chen Fan citations use Wanshi.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: Deng Wan versus Deng Wanshi.

sentences.zh: 又引惠棟說，謂唐諱「世」，故削之，猶「韓擒虎」為「韓擒」也。

translations.literal: Further cites Huidong: Tang taboo on shi cuts shi, like Han Qinhu becoming Han Qin.
translations.idiomatic: Collation explains Tang deletion of shi.

sentences.zh: 一六一九頁七行爵人必甄其德按：「必」原斗「以」，逕據汲本、殿本改正。

translations.literal: Page 1619 line 7: ennobling men must zhen their virtue—bi was dou yi, corrected per editions.
translations.idiomatic: Collation fixes bi graph.

sentences.zh: 一六二〇頁四行出入驕恣按：「驕」原斗「嬌」，逕據汲本、殿本改正。

translations.literal: Page 1620 line 4: coming and going arrogant zi—jiao was dou jiao, corrected per editions.
translations.idiomatic: Collation fixes jiao graph.

sentences.zh: 一六二〇頁一二行徐璆字孟玉殿本「玉」作「本」。

translations.literal: Page 1620 line 12: Xu Qiu styled Mengyu—Palace edition writes ben for yu.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: Mengyu versus Mengben.

sentences.zh: 按：集解引洪亮吉說，謂案先賢行狀作「孟平」，汝南先賢傳作「孟玉」。

translations.literal: Commentary: Hong Liangji cites Xianxian xingzhuang as Mengping and Ru nan xianxian zhuan as Mengyu.
translations.idiomatic: Collation cites two traditions for the style.

sentences.zh: 校補謂洪氏歷舉孟平、孟玉兩說，知所見本正文亦必作「孟本」。

translations.literal: Collation supplement says Hong listed both Mengping and Mengyu, so his text must have read Mengben; note should read 'also styled Mengyu.'
translations.idiomatic: Collation interprets Hong's note.

sentences.zh: 一六二一頁三行構造無端按：「構」原斗「構」，逕改正。

translations.literal: Page 1621 line 3: fabricating without basis—gou was miswritten as gou, corrected.
translations.idiomatic: Collation fixes duplicate graph error.

sentences.zh: 一六二一頁五行璆音仇按：殿本此下有「字孟玉」三字。

translations.literal: Page 1621 line 5: Qiu read like qiu—Palace adds three words 'styled Mengyu.'
translations.idiomatic: Collation: Palace adds style to phonetic note.

sentences.zh: 校補謂殿本就監本改刊，其正文作「字孟本」，注當是「一作字孟玉」，脫「一作」二字。

translations.literal: Collation supplement says Palace changed base text to Mengben and note should read 'also styled Mengyu' with yi zuo dropped.
translations.idiomatic: Collation on Palace variant.

sentences.zh: 一六二一頁六行寬裕*

translations.literal: Page 1621 line 6: generous*
translations.idiomatic: Collation Xu Shu note—

translations.literal: (zhuan)
translations.idiomatic: —zhuan gloss.

sentences.zh: **[博]*學據汲本、殿本改。

translations.literal: **[bo]* learning—emended per Ji and Palace.
translations.idiomatic: Collation restores bo for learning.
